name: azure-ai-voicelive-py
description: Build real-time voice AI applications using Azure AI Voice Live SDK (azure-ai-voicelive). Use this skill when creating Python applications that need real-time bidirectional audio communication with Azure AI, including voice assistants, voice-enabled chatbots, real-time speech-to-speech translation, voice-driven avatars, or any WebSocket-based audio streaming with AI models. Supports Server VAD (Voice Activity Detection), turn-based conversation, function calling, MCP tools, avatar integration, and transcription.
capabilities: Set up Azure AI Voice Live SDK with authentication (DefaultAzureCredential or API key)
Configure real-time bidirectional WebSocket audio communication
Implement audio input streaming with base64 PCM16 encoding
Handle voice activity detection (VAD) and turn-taking logic
Process model responses including audio, transcripts, and function calls
Manage conversation history and session configuration
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Documentation skill providing reference material for Azure AI Voice Live SDK. Static findings are false positives - scanner detected markdown code fences as shell commands, placeholder URLs as network calls, and example code patterns as security issues. No executable code or malicious behavior present.
valueStatement: Create interactive voice AI applications with real-time bidirectional audio streaming using Azure AI Voice Live SDK. This skill provides complete reference documentation for building production-ready voice assistants.
userTitle: Build Real-Time Voice AI Apps with Azure
limitations: Requires valid Azure Cognitive Services subscription and endpoint
Audio processing requires microphone hardware and proper sample rate configuration
Voice selection limited to Azure-supported voices (alloy, echo, shimmer, etc.)

useCases.title: Voice Assistant Development
useCases.description: Build interactive voice assistants that respond to user speech in real-time using Azure AI services

useCases.targetUser: Enterprise developers building automated support systems
useCases.title: Customer Service Bots
useCases.description: Create conversational AI agents for automated customer support with natural voice interaction

useCases.title: Accessibility Tools
useCases.description: Develop voice-controlled interfaces for accessibility applications

promptTemplates.title: Basic Voice Connection
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to set up a basic Azure AI Voice Live connection using DefaultAzureCredential with Python

promptTemplates.title: Audio Streaming Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Python example that streams audio from microphone to Azure Voice Live and plays back responses

promptTemplates.title: Session Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I configure session parameters like voice selection, modalities, and turn detection for a voice assistant?

promptTemplates.title: Event Handling Pattern
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a complete event handling pattern for Azure Voice Live that processes audio transcripts, function calls, and handles interruptions

bestPractices: Use DefaultAzureCredential instead of API keys for production environments
Configure appropriate turn detection settings based on use case (server_vad or semantic_vad)
Handle connection errors and implement reconnection logic for robust applications
Use appropriate audio formats matching your hardware capabilities (PCM16 at 24kHz default)
antiPatterns: Avoid hardcoding API keys in source code - use environment variables or Azure Key Vault
Do not skip error handling for connection drops and API errors
Avoid blocking operations in the event loop - use async/await properly
Do not assume continuous audio streaming - implement proper buffering and flow control

faq.question: What is Azure AI Voice Live SDK?
faq.answer: Azure AI Voice Live SDK is a Python library for building real-time bidirectional voice AI applications. It enables audio streaming to and from Azure AI services for speech interaction.

faq.question: Do I need an Azure subscription?
faq.answer: Yes, you need an Azure Cognitive Services subscription with the Speech service enabled. You will need the endpoint URL and appropriate authentication credentials.

faq.question: What audio formats are supported?
faq.answer: Azure Voice Live supports PCM16 (default at 24kHz), PCM16-8000hz, PCM16-16000hz, g711_ulaw, and g711_alaw formats.

faq.question: How does turn detection work?
faq.answer: Turn detection uses Voice Activity Detection (VAD) to detect when the user stops speaking. You can use server_vad (default), azure_semantic_vad (smarter), or disable it for manual turn control.

faq.question: Can I use custom voices?
faq.answer: Yes, Azure supports custom voices through AzureCustomVoice and AzurePersonalVoice models in addition to built-in voices like alloy, echo, and shimmer.

faq.question: Is this suitable for production use?
faq.answer: Yes, Azure AI Voice Live is designed for production applications. Use DefaultAzureCredential for secure authentication and implement proper error handling for reliable operation.
